title: Regime-Conditional Bayesian Calibration on OOF Logits
summary: Prior research already pointed toward calibration/blending, but a specific non-parametric post-processor is still untested here.
rationale: Prior research already pointed toward calibration/blending, but a specific non-parametric post-processor is still untested here. Bayesian Binning into Quantiles and related Bayesian/nonparametric calibration methods are designed to improve probability quality as a post-processing layer, which is useful when blending heterogeneous specialists whose raw score scales differ.
implementation_hint: Collect OOF logits from the main tabular model plus any specialists, then fit calibration only on OOF predictions, not in-fold scores. Test one global calibrator and separate calibrators for dry vs wet compounds or early vs late race, then average calibrated probabilities rank-safely if AUC holds.
expected_effect: Small but real gain from cleaner tail probabilities and better combination of heterogeneous specialists, especially if public scoring is sensitive to overconfident extremes.
risk: Calibration can flatten useful ranking signal if applied on noisy folds or very small regimes; it should be validated by AUC, not just calibration loss.
